# Ramadan and religious calendar — tone presets (MENA marketing)

Synthetic guidance only; align sends with client-approved religious advisory where required.

## Ramadan

- **Tone:** Respectful, calm, community-oriented; avoid loud humour during solemn imagery or charity-first messaging.
- **Scheduling:** Prefer **after iftar** or mid-morning for non-fasting audiences (always confirm segment). Avoid noisy promotional pushes at **maghrib** unless brief explicitly schedules iftar-themed offers.
- **Copy:** Welcome Ramadan explicitly only if brand has authentic seasonal relevance; avoid exploitation of charity angles.

## Eid al-Fitr / Eid al-Adha

- Short congratulations (`كل عام وأنتم بخير`, `عيد سعيد`) when brand culturally participates year-round or runs verified seasonal campaigns.
- Double-check **offer validity dates** vs lunar calendar uncertainty — phrase as "خلال أيام العيد" when exact Gregorian mapping varies.

## Mouled / Ashura / significant holy days

- No humour that trivializes religious figures or rituals.
- Alcohol, nightlife, or adult-positioning brands: **extra restraint** on religious days per market norms.

## Respectful-marketing guardrails

- Do not shame fasting/non-fasting audiences.
- Charity tie-ins require **verified mechanics** (no invented orphan counts or donation percentages).
